definition: A client-supplied token that lets the server detect and ignore duplicate requests so a retry is safe
A migration pattern that adds the new schema before removing the old so deploys roll back safely
A background job that migrates existing rows to a new schema shape after an expand step
Service Level Objective; the target reliability a service commits to
The allowed amount of failure under an SLO; spending it gates risky deploys
The scope of users or systems a change or failure can affect
Releasing to a fraction of instances at a time with a soak between batches
A short wait after a deploy batch to confirm health before continuing
A long-lived single-use token exchanged for a new short-lived session token
A memory-hard password hashing algorithm used to store credentials safely
An isolated customer account; all data and limits are scoped per tenant
A holding queue for messages that failed processing after all retries
